wedding_guest: Ceremony guest silhouettes with midi lengths and polished tailored lines
Crepe, chiffon, and satin blends suited to celebratory daytime dressing
Semi-formal dress code with refined restraint and non-bridal presentation
Balanced coverage, softened drape, and discreet ornament for wedding attendance
Social role: invited attendee dressing respectfully for a formal union
party_night_out: Body-skimming or abbreviated silhouettes built for energetic nightlife settings
Sequins, coated jersey, leather, and metallic knits with visual impact
After-dark dressiness with bold exposure and club-oriented confidence
Cutouts, shine, and high-contrast styling that read loud under evening lights
Social context: crowded late-night venues, dancing, and expressive party energy
work_office: Structured office silhouettes built from blazers, straight trousers, and sheath shapes
Gabardine, wool suiting, poplin, and compact knits with restrained finish
Business-appropriate formality calibrated for professional daytime environments
Clean closures, practical coverage, and low-ornament construction for workplace norms
Social context: desk-to-meeting dressing with competence and restraint
formal_event: Full-length evening silhouettes and tuxedo-inspired shapes for gala environments
Silk, velvet, satin, and fine wool with elevated evening surface quality
High ceremonial formality aligned with black-tie or gala expectations
Architectural drape, controlled embellishment, and precise finishing for formal attendance
Social context: red-carpet, banquet, or staged evening occasion dressing
casual_everyday: Relaxed daily silhouettes built from denim, tees, knits, and simple dresses
Cotton jersey, washed denim, soft fleece, and lightweight knit textures
Low-formality dressing centered on comfort, repetition, and routine wearability
Easy layering, minimal shaping, and practical utility details for daily movement
Social context: errands, commuting, coffee runs, and informal everyday life
date_night: Softly defined silhouettes that feel composed, close-fitting, and evening-oriented
Satin, smooth crepe, fine rib knits, and delicate lace accents
Moderate evening formality with intimacy rather than public spectacle
Subtle contouring, refined finishes, and controlled reveal for personal polish
Social context: one-to-one evening plans, dinners, or intimate outings
winter_outerwear: Layer-friendly outer silhouettes including long coats, puffers, and insulated parkas
Heavy wool, quilted fill, technical shell, and dense twill cold-weather materials
Utility-led formality centered on climate protection rather than event protocol
High collars, storm plackets, padding, and extended coverage for thermal defense
Social context: cold-weather transit, outdoor exposure, and winter protection needs
smart_casual: Mixed-formality silhouettes pairing relaxed separates with lightly tailored pieces
Twill, merino knits, chambray, suede, and brushed cotton for refined ease
Mid-level dress code between office polish and off-duty informality
Neat proportions, softened structure, and deliberate styling without rigid suiting
Social context: dinners, casual meetings, and polished but relaxed gatherings
navidad_dinner: Festive dinner silhouettes with softened tailoring, knit dresses, and polished separates
Velvet touches, brushed wools, satin blouses, and warm textured seasonal fabrics
Seasonal family-dinner formality that feels elevated without gala strictness
Layered warmth, tasteful sheen, and composed finishing suited to indoor holiday meals
Social context: Christmas dining, family hosting, and celebratory evening gathering
nochevieja_nye: Celebratory midnight silhouettes with sharper lines, shorter hems, or dramatic tailoring
Sequined surfaces, metallic textiles, satin shine, and party-weight evening materials
Festive high-energy dressiness geared toward countdown events and year-end parties
Sparkle, sharper contrast, and statement finishing designed for nightlife visibility
Social context: New Year's Eve countdown, champagne toasts, and midnight celebration
halloween: Darkly styled silhouettes with sharpened shapes and theatrical but wearable proportions
Leather, mesh, velvet, lace, and coated fabrics with moody surface depth
Event-specific evening dressing with stylized drama rather than formal protocol
Corsetry cues, layered black pieces, and eerie detailing with fashion-first restraint
Social context: Halloween parties requiring atmospheric, costume-adjacent fashion dressing
oktoberfest: Traditional festival silhouettes with fitted bodices, full skirts, or rustic tailored separates
Cotton poplin, sturdy twill, felted wool, and textured folk-inspired trims
Festive daytime dress code rooted in regional celebration rather than evening glamour
Apron layering, checked accents, and structured waist definition for beer-hall styling
Social context: Oktoberfest tents, folk celebration, and heritage-inspired social dressing
